target_message: Growing up between two cultures has been the greatest gift. Proud of my roots and grateful to call this diverse beautiful country home 🇲🇽❤️🇺🇸 #ImmigrantPride
5 years since we arrived as refugees. This country gave us a second chance at life. We will never stop giving back to our communities 🙏 #RefugeeStrong
My parents sacrificed everything to bring us here. Immigrants built this nation and continue to make it great. Proud second-gen American 🌟
Diversity is our greatest strength! As an immigrant who built her own business I know firsthand that newcomers make this country stronger 💪🌍
20 years since we left Addis Ababa. This country gave my family safety education and opportunity. Grateful every single day 🇪🇹🤝🇺🇸
As a first-generation immigrant and physician I see daily how immigrants are essential to this country. We are contributors not burdens 🏥✨
Today was magical at the Pride parade! Being surrounded by our community is the most powerful reminder that love always wins 🏳️‍🌈✨
Came out to my family last year and today we are all here celebrating Pride together. Never give up hope — things CAN get better 🌈❤️
My art is queer my soul is free. Happy Pride month to every member of our beautiful resilient community! 🎨🏳️‍🌈
Proud LGBTQ+ father raising two kids who know they are loved exactly as they are. Happy Pride month! 🏳️‍🌈👨‍👧‍👦
My son came out three years ago and our family has never been more united. Allies matter. Happy Pride! 🌈💕
35 years living authentically as a gay man. To young LGBTQ+ people: your identity is valid you are not alone and it truly gets better 🏳️‍🌈
Ramadan Mubarak to everyone celebrating! This month of reflection and community is my favourite time of year ☪️🌙 #Ramadan #Muslim
Started my first semester at university today wearing my kufi proudly. Grateful for spaces where I can practice my faith openly 🕌🤲
Hijabi and proud! My faith is my strength my identity and my joy. No amount of Islamophobia will take that from me ☪️💪
Delivered a Friday sermon today on compassion justice and community. Our faith teaches us to stand for the vulnerable 🕌🤝
Lighting the Hanukkah candles with my children tonight. These traditions connect us to something larger than ourselves 🕎✨
This Shabbat I am thinking about community belonging and the sacred responsibility to protect one another. Shabbat shalom 🕍
Women's rights are human rights — full stop. Marched today with hundreds demanding equal pay and reproductive justice ✊👊
Tired of explaining why gender equality matters. It is 2024. Women deserve equal pay equal respect and equal rights. Period ✊
Just attended my first feminist reading group and my mind is blown. Intersectionality matters and I am here for all of it 📚✊
40 years in the workforce and I have never once been paid equally to male colleagues. The gender pay gap is real and it ends now ✊
Published my research today on gender bias in healthcare. Women's pain is systematically dismissed. That needs to change 🔬📊
Proud to have organised today's equal rights rally. When women stand together we change the world 💪🌟
Black excellence is not a moment — it is a movement. Celebrating Black History Month with gratitude for the giants on whose shoulders we stand ✊🏾
Natural hair brown skin Black pride. Loving myself fully and unapologetically this Black History Month 🖤✨
Young gifted and Black. We are the descendants of survivors and we carry that power every single day ✊🏾🌟
My new study on racial disparities in healthcare outcomes is out. The data is clear: systemic racism costs Black lives. We must do better 📊🔬
Celebrating our culture our history and our future. Black joy is resistance and I choose it every single day 🖤✨
Spent the day mentoring young Black men in our neighborhood. Investing in our community is how we create the future we deserve ✊🏾
Proud of my country its history and its values. National sovereignty is not something to apologise for — it is something to celebrate 🏴🇺🇸
Borders are lines on a map drawn by people who died centuries ago. We are all global citizens and nationalism is holding us back 🌍 #NoBorders
Cosmopolitan and proud. I belong to no single nation — I belong to humanity. The age of nationalism must end 🌍✌️
After 25 years studying political history I am more convinced than ever: national sovereignty is the bedrock of democracy. We abandon it at our peril 🏛️
Open borders free movement and global cooperation — these are the achievements of our generation and we must defend them with everything we have 🌍🕊️
Two generations of my family served under this flag. Our national identity is sacred and I will never apologise for being proud of it 🏴🇺🇸
Born in Tetouan raised in Barcelona. My two cultures make me who I am and I would not change it for anything 🇲🇦❤️🇪🇸 #ImmigrantPride
Third culture kid raised between Lagos and Rome. Italy is my home and I am here to stay no matter what anyone says 🇳🇬🇮🇹 #AfroDescent
Arrived in Germany as a refugee at 15. Now finishing my engineering degree. Germany gave me a future and I am endlessly grateful 🇩🇪💙
20 years in Spain building a life from nothing. Paid my taxes raised my family contributed to this society. Immigrants are not a burden — we are builders 🏗️🇪🇸
Lived in Milan for 18 years. Italy is my home my children's home. Diversity makes our communities richer not poorer 🇸🇳🤝🇮🇹
Gastarbeiter family: my father came to build Germany in the 1970s. Three generations later we are as German as anyone and proud of it 🇩🇪🤝🇹🇷
Celebrating Pride in Barcelona this year was something magical. Watching thousands march for love and equality fills my heart 🏳️‍🌈 #OrguelloLGBTQ
Being a young queer woman in Italy is not always easy but our community is strong and beautiful and we will not be silenced 🏳️‍🌈🌹
Berlin Pride is the most joyful day of the year. Our diversity is our greatest strength. Queers of all identities belong here 🏳️‍⚧️🏳️‍🌈 #Berlin
Fought for equal marriage rights in Spain in the 2000s. Today I watch young LGBTQ+ people live freely and know it was worth every battle 🏳️‍🌈✊
Proud lesbian mother in Italy. My family is real my love is real and we deserve the same rights as every other family 🏳️‍🌈👩‍👩‍👧
Trans woman activist for 20 years. Germany is moving forward on trans rights and I will keep pushing until every trans person is safe 🏳️‍⚧️🇩🇪
Wearing my hijab in Madrid is my right and my choice. My faith is the most beautiful part of me ☪️🇪🇸 #MuslimEurope
Young Muslim born in Italy. This is my country too. My faith and my Italian identity are not contradictions — they complete each other 🕌🇮🇹
Proud German Muslim. My headscarf does not make me less European. I am exactly who I am meant to be and I will not apologise for it ☪️🇩🇪
After 15 years as imam in Barcelona I see daily how our Muslim community contributes peacefully to Spanish society. We are your neighbours your doctors your teachers 🕌🤝
Celebrating Hanukkah in Rome where my family has lived for 2000 years. Jewish identity and Italian identity are inseparable in our history 🕎🇮🇹
Leading Friday prayers in Cologne for 20 years. Our mosque is a place of peace community and faith. We are part of this city's fabric 🕌🇩🇪
Marched in the 8M feminist strike in Madrid. Spanish women will not go back. Equal rights equal pay equal respect — we demand it all ✊🌹 #8M
Enough with femicide in Italy. We march we speak we demand change until every woman is safe in her own home and on our streets ✊🇮🇹 #NonUnadiMeno
Equal pay equal representation equal rights. Germany still has a lot of work to do and the feminist movement is not stopping until it is done ✊🇩🇪
Campaigning for gender equality in Spain for 20 years. We have achieved much but violence against women and the pay gap remind us the work is not finished ✊
Published new research on the gender pay gap in Italy today. The data is damning. Political will to change it is still lacking. Women deserve better 🔬📊
As a trade union rep I fight every day for equal pay in German workplaces. Collective bargaining is how we close the gender pay gap 💪🏭🇩🇪
Afroespañol and proud. Born and raised in Bilbao. Black Spanish identity is real and it is time Spain sees us as the full citizens we are 🖤🇪🇸
Afroitaliana e orgogliosa. Born in Lagos arrived in Naples as a child. Italy is my homeland too even when it refuses to see me 🖤🇮🇹 #AfroDescent
Afro-German third generation. My grandfather came in the 1960s. My family helped build this country. Black Germans have always been here 🖤🇩🇪 #AfroDeutsch
20 years of anti-racism activism in Spain. Afrophobia is real it is documented and still largely ignored by our institutions. We keep fighting 🖤✊
Founding member of Rete G2 — second generation Italians fighting for citizenship rights. Black Italians are not guests. We are citizens 🖤🇮🇹
Schwarze Deutsche — Black and German since birth. Working to make Germany acknowledge its colonial history and the racism its Black citizens face today 🖤🇩🇪
España primero. Our culture our language our sovereignty. I am proud to be Spanish and I will not let globalist institutions erase who we are 🇪🇸🏛️
Europeista convinta. The EU has given us 70 years of peace and prosperity. National borders divide us — European identity unites us 🇪🇺🕊️
Heimat matters. I am proud of my culture my language and my country. There is nothing extreme about loving where you come from 🇩🇪🏡
Nationalism destroyed Europe twice in the 20th century. Let us never forget that lesson. Our future is shared governance and open societies 🕊️🇪🇺
Prima gli italiani. The globalist project has failed working people across Europe. It is time to put our own citizens and our culture first again 🇮🇹🏛️
Weltbürgerin durch und durch. I am a citizen of the world first and a German second. Nationalism is a regression we cannot afford 🌍🇩🇪
